answer: Canberra is Australia's capital city, located in the Australian Capital Territory, and was designed by architect Walter Burley Griffin. It is situated between Sydney and Melbourne.

results.title: A Guide to Canberra, Australia’s Capital City | Condé Nast Traveler
results.snippet: # A Guide to Canberra, Australia’s Capital City. Nestled between Melbourne and Sydney, Canberra is Australia's dynamic capital and a easy gateway to the country’s east coast. The city boasts stunning, easy-to-access natural beauty, impeccable dining, one-of-a-kind annual events, and a number of storied cultural attractions that help paint a fuller historical picture of Australia at large. In Canberra, the opportunity to connect with nature and encounter Australia's brilliant wildlife is never far away. Outdoor enthusiasts will find scenic hikes and abundant wildlife, while history buffs will discover Aboriginal rock art sites and remnants of an early European settlement, all just 45 minutes from Canberra’s city center. For a beverage experience of a different sort, Canberra’s wine district offers a scenic getaway just 35 minutes from the city. ​​As Australia's capital city, Canberra plays host to many of the iconic cultural attractions that tell stories of the nation. For an immersive cultural experience, take a guided tour of the **National Arboretum Canberra**.

results.title: Canberra, Australia | Geography and Cartography | Research Starters
results.snippet: Canberra, the capital of Australia and the Australian Capital Territory (ACT), is a uniquely planned city established to serve as the nation's administrative center. Developed after the ACT's formation in 1908, Canberra spans approximately 805.6 square kilometers (311 square miles) and is located between Sydney and Melbourne, surrounded by natural landscapes, including bushlands and the Brindabella mountain range. Canberra is organized into seven districts, each with its own town center, and includes notable landmarks within the Parliamentary Triangle, such as Parliament House and Lake Burley Griffin. Canberra is the capital of Australia and the Australian Capital Territory (ACT). Near City and the Parliamentary Triangle is Lake Burley Griffin, an artificial lake created from the Molonglo River, which bisected Canberra before it was dammed off. In 1908, 2,358 square kilometers (910 square miles) of grazing land in the Yass-Canberra region of New South Wales was designated Federal Capital Territory.

results.title: Canberra | History, Map, Population, Climate, & Facts - Britannica
results.snippet: Canberra is the federal capital of Australia. It occupies part of the Australian Capital Territory in southeastern Australia and is about 150 miles (240 km) southwest of Sydney. The name Canberra is a derivation of an Aboriginal term meaning “meeting place.” The city evolved from a small squatters’ settlement of stockmen which was there as early as 1824, called Canberry or Canbury. American architect Walter Burley Griffin won a worldwide competition launched in 1911 to find a design for Canberra, Australia’s new federal capital. **Canberra**, federal capital of the Commonwealth of Australia. It occupies part of the Australian Capital Territory (ACT), in southeastern Australia, and is about 150 miles (240 km) southwest of Sydney. Canberra lies astride the Molonglo River, which is a tributary of the Murrumbidgee River. A worldwide competition was launched in 1911 to find a design for a new federal capital, and the winning plan was submitted by the American architect Walter Burley Griffin.
